title: refactor(social): collapse three near-duplicate buffer publish functions
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Unify publish_linkedin/twitter/instagram bodies into one config-driven publish_post(label, services, max_chars, on_overflow, multi_image, metadata_fn)
- Keep publish_*_post as thin wrappers so publish_daily.py/publish_weekly.py imports + publish_fn(post_data, token) signature are unchanged (no caller edits)
- Encode real per-service divergences in config: LinkedIn rejects over-limit (1248), Twitter (280)/Instagram (2200) truncate to max-3+'...'; twitter falls back to 'x'; Instagram sends all images ({'photos': urls}) + carousel metadata
- Net -26 lines; removes ~130 lines of duplicated channel/text/media/schedule boilerplate

Risks:
- No Python tests exist for buffer_publish; verified manually via runtime trace that LinkedIn over-limit still returns False (rejects, never calls Buffer) and Instagram still sends ALL images + carousel metadata (single/stored-carousel/multi cases), plus twitter->x fallback, empty-text, and missing-channel paths.

_~130 net lines removed · biome ✓ · No Python test suite exists for buffer_publish. Ran `python3 -m py_compile buffer_publish. ✓_

gist.headline: Tidying the social hive
gist.blurb: Cleaned up our internal social media publishing scripts, making the code leaner and easier to maintain.
gist.summary: Refactored the internal Buffer publishing script to unify LinkedIn, Twitter, and Instagram posting logic into a single configuration-driven function.
gist.impact: This reduces code duplication by 130 lines and makes the social media pipeline easier to maintain without changing any external behavior.
gist.visual_concept: A cute pixel robot organizing three different colored envelopes (representing Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram) into a single neat filing cabinet drawer. The bee mascot is helping by carrying one of the envelopes.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head organizing blue, pink, and blue envelopes into a single neat filing cabinet. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby carrying a tiny envelope. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
